name: Add action list support to Task

plannedActions.name: Phase 1: Refactor FormaCollection to eliminate createForParent requirement
Update IFormaItemClass interface: change constructor signature to accept cfg parameter, remove createForParent requirement
Update FormaCollection.addItem(): create related UUID64 internally using UUID64.createRelation(this.parentId), call constructor with cfg
plannedActions.test: verify addItem creates items with related IDs

plannedActions.name: Update FormaCollection documentation: remove createForParent references from comments
Update test/forma-collection.ts: remove TestItem.createForParent(), update constructor to handle cfg.id
Run full test suite: npm run test to verify FormaCollection refactor
Phase 2: Add Task.actions field using refactored FormaCollection
Update Task.ts imports: add FormaCollection and Action imports
Add readonly actions: FormaCollection<Action> field to Task class; initialize in constructor from optional cfg.actions array
plannedActions.test: verify constructor accepts actions array, actions property returns FormaCollection

plannedActions.name: Update Task.SCHEMA: add actions field using FormaCollection.schemaOf(Action)
plannedActions.test: verify schema includes actions field with correct Avro structure

plannedActions.name: Add comprehensive tests: constructor with actions, FormaCollection CRUD via task.actions.*, fuzzy ID matching, Avro serialize/deserialize with actions
Verify patch() ignores actions field (only updates name/summary)
plannedActions.test: test patch() does not modify actions

plannedActions.name: Run full test suite: npm run test to verify Task.actions integration

references.text: CURRENT IFormaItemClass (lines 17-20): export interface IFormaItemClass { new (): IFormaItem; createForParent(parentId: UUID64, cfg: any): IFormaItem; }
ZERO production classes implement createForParent() - Action, Task, Clock all lack it. Only test classes have it. Safe to refactor.
CRITICAL: createForParent() is unnecessary boilerplate - only creates UUID64.createRelation(parentId) and passes to constructor. FormaCollection should handle this internally.
File: test/kafka.integration.ts - TestItem.createForParent() at lines 22-24 (REMOVE THIS METHOD)
CURRENT addItem() (lines 80-84): addItem(cfg: any): T { const item = this.#ItemClass.createForParent(this.parentId, cfg) as T; this.#items.push(item); return item; }
File: src/forma-collection.ts - IFormaItemClass interface at lines 17-20, addItem() at lines 80-84, documentation comments at lines 34, 40, 76-78
NEW IFormaItemClass: export interface IFormaItemClass { new (cfg?: any): IFormaItem; }
File: doc/tansu.md - MyItem.createForParent() example at lines 141-154 (REMOVE FROM DOCS)
File: test/forma-collection.ts - TestItem.createForParent() at lines 28-32 (REMOVE THIS METHOD)
Refactor scope: 4 files (forma-collection.ts, 2 test files, 1 doc), ~15 lines. Small, low-risk change.

DECISION: Direct exposure pattern - expose FormaCollection as readonly public field. API: forma.actions.addItem() instead of forma.addAction(). Zero boilerplate, Kafka-compatible (FormaCollection methods are controlled mutation points for future event emission)
FormaCollection.schemaOf(Action) - generates Avro array schema for typed collections (ActionFormaCollection). Returns { name, namespace, type: 'array', items: Action.SCHEMA }
New addItem() pattern: const id = UUID64.createRelation(this.parentId); return new this.#ItemClass({ ...cfg, id }) as T;
FormaCollection API: addItem(cfg), deleteItem(id), getItem(id), patchItem(id, cfg), moveItem(id, opts), items(filter?), size - all accept FuzzyId for fuzzy matching
New IFormaItemClass interface: { new (cfg?: any): IFormaItem } - constructor accepts cfg, no createForParent requirement
Constructor pattern: new Task({ actions: [{ status: 'todo' }, ...] }) - FormaCollection initialized from cfg.actions array, addItem() creates related IDs automatically
DECISION: patch() ignores actions field; use forma.actions.addItem/deleteItem/patchItem for mutations (Kafka-friendly granular updates)
Identifiable.SCHEMA - static getter returning Avro record schema with id field (UUID64.avroSchema). Namespace: scvoice.nameforma
Kafka integration: FormaCollection methods (addItem, deleteItem, patchItem) are future event emission points per transactional outbox pattern (see T_AZ008zeCc)
Action class - extends Forma with status field (todo|done), has SCHEMA. No createForParent() needed after refactor.
FormaCollection class (forma-collection.ts) - encapsulates array, CRUD methods use Identifiable.idFilter() for fuzzy ID matching
Forma base class - extends Identifiable with name/summary, Avro schema, patch() method
NEW addItem(): addItem(cfg: any = {}): T { const id = UUID64.createRelation(this.parentId); const item = new this.#ItemClass({ ...cfg, id }) as T; this.#items.push(item); return item; }

summary: Phase 1: Refactor FormaCollection to eliminate createForParent boilerplate. Phase 2: Add readonly actions: FormaCollection<Action> field to Task with Avro schema support, direct FormaCollection API exposure, and comprehensive tests
